title: Orchestrator-Worker Patterns
concept: Designing a supervisor agent that coordinates tasks, delegates to specialized workers, and synthesizes results.

exercises.prompt: What is the main advantage of using multiple specialized agents rather than one big agent?

options.text: Multiple agents can work together faster.
One agent always makes better decisions.

exercises.explain: Using multiple specialized agents allows for faster and more efficient task completion by leveraging their individual strengths.

cards.title: Meet the Orchestrator!
cards.body: Think of an orchestra conductor coordinating musicians. The conductor tells each musician when to play, making beautiful music together!

cards.title: What is an Orchestrator?
cards.body: An orchestrator is a supervisor agent that oversees tasks and delegates them to specialized worker agents, ensuring everything runs smoothly.

cards.title: Orchestrator as a Team Coach
cards.body: Like a sports coach, the orchestrator sees the big picture, makes strategies, and assigns roles to players (workers) based on their best skills.

cards.title: Real-World Example
cards.body: Imagine a project manager (orchestrator) who tells software developers (workers) specific features to build, based on their expertise.

cards.title: Myth: One Agent Can Do It All!
cards.body: It's a myth that a single agent can outperform many specialized ones. Complex tasks require collaboration!

cards.title: Orchestrator vs. Workers
cards.body: The orchestrator manages, while workers execute tasks for efficiency!

exercises.prompt: What is a key role of the orchestrator in a multi-agent system?

options.text: To coordinate and delegate tasks to workers.
To sit back and watch the workers do everything.
To only focus on one specific task.

exercises.explain: The orchestrator's main role is to coordinate and delegate tasks, ensuring that all specialized agents work effectively together.

exercises.prompt: An orchestrator agent works alone without any worker agents.
exercises.explain: This statement is false because the orchestrator relies on worker agents to perform specific tasks.

exercises.prompt: The orchestrator combines the results from different _______ agents to provide a final output.
exercises.sentence: The orchestrator combines the results from different worker agents to provide a final output.
exercises.explain: The correct term here is 'worker', as orchestrators work with specialized agents.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes the function of the orchestrator in a multi-agent system?

options.text: To ensure each worker does a different task.
To handle all tasks by itself.

exercises.explain: The orchestrator's primary function is to ensure that specialized workers complete distinct tasks efficiently.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of using an orchestrator-worker model in a multi-agent system?

options.text: It reduces the number of agents needed.
It allows tasks to be completed faster and more accurately.
It eliminates the need for collaboration.
It makes the system more chaotic.

exercises.explain: The primary benefit is that this model enhances speed and accuracy by allowing workers to focus on their strengths while the orchestrator coordinates their efforts.
